title: elaeocarpus hortensis
intro: Elaeocarpus hortensis, also known as the blueberry ash or blue olive, is a flowering plant species native to Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The leaves and fruits of Elaeocarpus hortensis have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, skin infections, and digestive issues, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation and promoting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health ailments.
